shared_properties: The layer spans the full screen and uses warm brown values that stay behind gameplay elements.
The center corridor stays more open than the side masses to protect obstacle readability.
The silhouette remains static so scrolling motion comes from code-side parallax only.
differences: This layer establishes distant treeline depth with broad soft crowns and limited contrast.
Open space through the middle preserves a readable fall corridor for the player leaf and branch gates.
The art is tuned for slow parallax movement rather than hard foreground framing.

reference_images.label: leaf_beat_gdd
reference_images.purpose: Grounds the core loop, timing windows, obstacle pacing, UI layout, and Bevy target constraints.

reference_images.label: leaf_beat_moodboard
reference_images.purpose: Grounds the sepia autumn atmosphere, tree silhouettes, and warm color layering.

bevy_constraints.bundle_strategy: SpriteBundle parallax entity with a low movement factor tied to camera descent.
bevy_constraints.asset_loading: Load world/art_000012.png during boot and apply a 0.3x vertical parallax factor during gameplay.
runtime_notes: Scroll the texture at 0.3x of the player fall speed so distant depth cues remain calm and readable.
Keep tree crowns broad and low-contrast so the layer never competes with branch obstacles in the active corridor.
Tile or loop the layer seamlessly if the stage length exceeds one screen height in capture tools.

context.usage: Slow parallax tree band that frames the fall corridor without obscuring obstacles.

shape.silhouette: Soft distant treeline with rounded crowns and open negative space around the central corridor.

acceptance_criteria: PNG canvas is exactly 1280x720px with the central 320px corridor kept visually lighter than the side masses.
Tree crowns avoid razor-thin twigs so the parallax layer does not shimmer during scrolling.
The left and right masses balance the frame while leaving the middle corridor readable for obstacle scan.
